mission.title: Install and Configure Kube Ovn on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kube-OVN integrates OVN-based Network Virtualization with Kubernetes, providing enhanced networking capabilities such as multi-tenancy, VPC support, and seamless VM live migration. Installing Kube-OVN allows you to leverage advanced networking features in your Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Add the Kube-OVN Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Kube-OVN Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kube-ovn https://charts.kube-ovn.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kube-OVN using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kube-OVN in the kube-system namespace with the specified version:
```bash
helm install kube-ovn kube-ovn/kube-ovn-v2 --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version 1.16.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the Kube-OVN pods to ensure they are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Kube-OVN deployment to set resource limits for the pods:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kube-ovn -n kube-system
``` 
Add the following under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up monitoring with Prometheus
steps.description:
Enable Prometheus monitoring by configuring the necessary annotations in the Kube-OVN deployment:
```bash
kubectl annotate deployment kube-ovn -n kube-system prometheus.io/scrape=true
kubectl annotate deployment kube-ovn -n kube-system prometheus.io/port=9100
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have Kube-OVN pods running in the kube-system namespace, and you will be able to verify the deployment and resource limits. Additionally, Prometheus monitoring will be set up for Kube-OVN.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kube-ovn https://charts.kube-ovn.io
helm repo update

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
